title: Road Service Technician (Lift Trucks)
summary: A material-handling equipment provider is hiring a road service technician for the Vancouver area, servicing lift trucks at customer sites from a company truck. $76K-$85K plus overtime, full benefits, pension, and a company truck, iPad and iPhone.
description:
A material-handling equipment provider is hiring a Road Service Technician for the Vancouver area. You'll service and repair lift trucks and carousels at customer sites, working from a company truck.

Setup:
- Field-based role across the Vancouver area
- Company truck, insurance and gas card provided
- Company iPad and iPhone
- Overtime paid after 44 hours worked per week

What you'll do:
- Inspect the operational integrity and condition of lift trucks
- Diagnose mechanical and electrical issues and recommend repairs to the customer
- Perform scheduled preventative maintenance
- Diagnose and order parts using technical manuals
- Document and analyze breakdowns to standard procedures
- Act as the front-line contact with the customer on site

Requirements:
- 3 to 5 years as a mechanical technician, or equivalent experience
- 310S (Automotive) or 421A (Heavy Duty) certification is an asset
- Knowledge of mechanical and electrical systems on battery and engine-powered material handling equipment
- Comfortable with hand tools, measuring devices, and electrical/electronic diagnostic tools
- Solid lockout/tagout knowledge
- Strong communication and customer-service skills
- Valid driver's license

Compensation and benefits:
- $76,000 to $85,000 per year plus overtime
- Benefits after 3 months, pension after 24 months
- Company truck, insurance, gas card, plus iPad and iPhone
- Incentive program and career development

title: Road Service Technician (Lift Trucks)
summary: A material-handling equipment provider is hiring a road service technician for Truro and surrounding areas, servicing lift trucks at customer sites from a company van. $65K-$75K plus overtime, full benefits, pension matching, tool and boot allowances, and a company truck, iPad and iPhone.
description:
A material-handling equipment provider is hiring a Road Service Technician for Truro and the surrounding area. You'll service and repair lift trucks and carousels at customer sites, working from a company service van.

Setup:
- Field-based role covering Truro, NS and surrounding areas
- Company truck, insurance and gas card provided
- Company iPad and iPhone
- Overtime paid after 44 hours worked per week
- You'll need a driveway to park the company van (Sprinter-sized; it cannot be parked on the street)

What you'll do:
- Inspect the operational integrity and condition of lift trucks
- Diagnose mechanical and electrical issues and recommend repairs to the customer
- Perform scheduled preventative maintenance
- Diagnose and order parts using technical manuals
- Document and analyze breakdowns to standard procedures
- Act as the front-line contact with the customer on site

Requirements:
- 3 to 5 years as a mechanical technician, or equivalent experience
- 310S (Automotive) or 421A (Heavy Duty) certification is an asset
- Knowledge of mechanical and electrical systems on battery and engine-powered material handling equipment
- Comfortable with hand tools, measuring devices, and electrical/electronic diagnostic tools
- Solid lockout/tagout knowledge
- Strong communication and customer-service skills
- Valid driver's license and a clear driver's abstract
- Comfortable lifting up to 50 lbs

Compensation and benefits:
- $65,000 to $75,000 per year plus overtime
- Benefits after 3 months, pension matching after 24 months
- Annual tool ($200) and boot ($125) allowances
- Company truck, insurance, gas card, plus iPad and iPhone
- Incentive program and career development

title: Internal Combustion Road Service Technician (Diesel)
summary: A material-handling equipment provider is hiring an internal combustion road service technician for the Mississauga and Milton area, servicing diesel and propane forklifts at customer sites. Up to $96K, full benefits, pension, tool and boot allowances, plus a company truck, iPad and iPhone. Diesel experience and a 310S, 310T or 421A license required.
description:
A material-handling equipment provider is hiring an Internal Combustion Road Service Technician for the Mississauga and Milton area. You'll service and repair diesel and propane internal combustion forklifts, including larger all-terrain units, mostly out in the yard at customer sites. Comfort working outdoors in all seasons is a must (PPE and winter gear provided).

Setup:
- Field-based role across the Mississauga and Milton area
- Company truck, insurance and gas card provided
- Company iPad and iPhone
- PPE and winter wear provided
- Overtime paid after 44 hours worked per week

What you'll do:
- Service and repair diesel and propane internal combustion forklifts, including larger all-terrain units
- Diagnose mechanical and electrical issues and carry out repairs
- Perform scheduled preventative maintenance
- Diagnose and order parts using technical manuals

Requirements:
- Licensed technician: 310S, 310T or 421A
- Diesel experience is required
- Comfortable working outdoors in all seasons
- Valid driver's license

Compensation and benefits:
- Up to $96,000 per year, based on licensing and experience
- Benefits after 3 months, pension after 24 months
- Annual tool and boot allowances
- Company truck, insurance, gas card, plus iPad and iPhone

title: Road Service Technician (Lift Trucks)
summary: A material-handling equipment provider is hiring road service technicians for the Greater Calgary area, servicing lift trucks at customer sites from a company truck. Day shift, $86K-$92K plus overtime, full benefits, pension, and tool and boot allowances.
description:
A material-handling equipment provider is hiring Road Service Technicians for the Greater Calgary area. You'll service and repair lift trucks and related equipment at customer sites, working from a company truck. Day shift, Monday to Friday, with a strong salary plus overtime.

Schedule and setup:
- 40 hours/week, Monday to Friday, day shift
- Overtime paid after 44 hours worked per week
- Company work truck and gas card provided (you'll need a spot to park the truck)

What you'll do:
- Inspect the operational integrity and condition of lift trucks
- Diagnose mechanical and electrical issues and recommend repairs to the customer
- Perform scheduled preventative maintenance
- Diagnose and order parts using technical manuals
- Document and analyze breakdowns to standard procedures
- Act as the front-line contact with the customer on site

Requirements:
- 3 to 5 years as a mechanical technician, or equivalent experience
- 310S (Automotive) or 421A (Heavy Duty) certification is an asset
- Industrial propane certification (ICE-IE-P) is an asset
- Knowledge of mechanical and electrical systems on battery and engine-powered material handling equipment
- Comfortable with hand tools, measuring devices, and electrical/electronic diagnostic tools
- Solid lockout/tagout knowledge
- Strong communication and customer-service skills
- Valid driver's license and a clean driver's abstract

Compensation and benefits:
- $86,000 to $92,000 per year plus overtime
- Benefits after 3 months, pension after 24 months
- Annual tool and boot allowance
- Company truck and gas card
- Incentive program and career development

title: Truck & Trailer Technician (Night Shift)
summary: A transportation company is hiring a licensed truck and trailer technician for night shift at its Calgary-area shop. Preventative-maintenance focus, strong hourly rate plus shift premium, full benefits, and apprentices are welcome.
description:
A transportation company is hiring a licensed Truck & Trailer Technician for its Calgary-area shop. This is a permanent night-shift role focused on preventative maintenance, keeping the fleet safe and on the road. The shop handles PM and inspections; major repairs go to the dealership.

Schedule:
- 4 x 10-hour shifts, Monday to Thursday, 4:00pm to 2:30am
- 24/5 shop operation

What you'll do:
- Preventative maintenance and repairs on trucks and trailers
- Diagnose issues found during inspections and carry out the correct repair
- Perform safety inspections (Safeties)
- Complete paperless work orders
- Support and mentor apprentices

Requirements:
- Licensed Tractor or Trailer Technician (Journeyman or Red Seal preferred)
- Qualified to perform Safeties
- Strong diagnostics and time management
- Basic computer skills for work order entry
- Class 5 driver's license
- Full work authorization (PR or citizen)
- Comfortable working unsupervised

Compensation and benefits:
- $35.00 to $38.00/hr plus $2.00/hr shift premium (flexible for strong candidates)
- Open to 3rd and 4th year apprentices
- RRSP matching
- Annual tool ($350) and boot ($150) allowances
- Flexible health and dental, group life and disability, AD&D
- Paid personal leave and potential overtime
- Employee and Family Assistance Program, plus career development

Mostly Freightliner, Mack, Volvo and Peterbilt equipment in a clean, well-run shop.

title: Bilingual Heavy Equipment Mechanic (Cranes)
summary: A long-established crane and heavy-equipment dealer is hiring a bilingual heavy equipment mechanic for its Laval shop. Shop-based work on cranes and lifting equipment with occasional field calls across Quebec, up to $48/hr, full benefits, pension, and plenty of overtime. French and English required.
description:
A long-established crane and heavy-equipment dealer is hiring a Bilingual Heavy Equipment Mechanic for its Laval shop. This is a permanent, mostly shop-based role with some field service calls to customer sites across Quebec (travel covered for longer trips). You'll work on cranes, boom trucks, heavy forklifts and other lifting equipment.

Bilingualism (French and English) is required for this role.

What you'll do:
- Pre-delivery inspections (PDI) and setting up new cranes
- Commercial inspections, diagnostics and repairs
- Preventative maintenance, from oil changes to major repairs
- Field service calls to customer sites across Quebec as needed

Requirements:
- Licensed Heavy Equipment Technician (Journeyman or Red Seal) preferred; final-year apprentices ready to challenge the exam will be considered
- Experience with heavy or industrial equipment such as cranes or heavy forklifts is a strong asset
- Comfortable reading electrical schematics and troubleshooting electrical systems (a big part of the crane side)
- Comfortable working at heights is an asset (tower crane work)
- Strong written and spoken communication in both French and English (this is a customer-facing role)

Compensation and benefits:
- Up to $48.00/hr based on experience
- Annual tool ($1,000) and boot ($250) allowances
- Benefits and pension plan
- Significant overtime available

Requirements:
- 7+ years of experience in the medium/heavy-duty truck industry
- Valid 310T license
- Prior dealership experience
- Demonstrated experience managing a service team

Compensation:
- $85,000 to $125,000 based on experience
- Matched pension and full health and dental benefits
- Day shift
title: Service Manager
summary: This role oversees the full service department, including the technician team, daily scheduling, customer relationships, and departmental financials. It is a permanent, on-site position with a family-owned dealership that has operated in the industry for several decades.

description:
SHIFT: Monday to Friday, 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM

We’re working with a well-established, industry-leading organization in the fleet services space, currently looking to add a licensed 310T Technician to their team.  This is a stable, well-run operation with a North American presence with strong leadership, consistent hours, and a solid compensation + benefits package.
The Role:
Perform diagnostics, repairs, and preventative maintenance on diesel trucks and heavy equipment

Comfortable working both independently and within a team

RRSP matching up to 5%
Employee stock purchase program (discounted rate)

Paid vacation
$1,200 annual tool allowance + boot allowance
Ongoing training: factory-level exposure
Defensive driving
Fleet systems training

Afternoon shift
Primarily shop-based with occasional field support
Clean, well-maintained facility
Strong focus on safety and team environment
Interested individuals are encouraged to submit your resume ASAP.
Although we like to thank all that apply, only qualified candidates will be contacted.
title: 310T Technician – Afternoon Shift (Shop-Based)

description:
Certus Technical Search is hiring a Dock & Door Technician to support a high-demand service operation, ensuring critical dock equipment remains safe, reliable, and fully operational. This role is with a national leader in industrial equipment sales and service.
 
This is a field-based role for a mechanically inclined technician who enjoys hands-on work, problem-solving, and working directly with customers. You’ll be responsible for servicing and maintaining dock equipment at client sites, ensuring safe and reliable operation in fast-paced industrial environments.
 
What will your day look like?
 
Respond to service calls and travel to customer sites across the region
Diagnose and repair dock equipment including overhead doors, dock levelers, seals, bumpers, and restraints
Perform preventative maintenance and safety inspections
Troubleshoot mechanical and basic electrical issues
Complete work orders and document service performed, parts used, and follow-up actions
Communicate clearly with customers regarding issues, solutions, and repair timelines
Ensure all work is completed in line with safety standards and service expectations
Build strong customer relationships through professional and reliable service
 
What do you need to qualify for this opportunity?
 
2+ year of experience in a mechanical or technician role (industrial, automotive, equipment, small engine or related)
Strong mechanical aptitude with basic electrical knowledge
Welding experience is a strong asset
Comfortable using hand tools, diagnostic tools, and measuring devices
Ability to read and follow technical procedures
Strong communication and customer service skills
Valid driver’s license and ability to travel to client sites
 
What can we offer you?
An annual salary in the $70,000 – 90,000 range plus O/T (higher end of the range reserved for direct Dock Door mechanical experience)
Comprehensive benefits package
Company pension matching
Company vehicle, gas call, tool & boot allowance provided
Ongoing training and development
Long-term career growth with a stable, well-established organization
A supportive environment where you’re trusted to manage your work and deliver results
 
If you’re a mechanically inclined technician looking for a field-based role with strong training, stability, and growth potential, submit your resume to learn more.
 
Although we’d like to thank all that apply, only qualified candidates will be contacted.

title: Fleet Maintenance Manager
description:
Certus Technical Search is working with a major transit operator to hire a Fleet Maintenance Manager for their Concord location.
This is a key leadership role where you will directly impact fleet reliability, service delivery, and overall operational performance. Leading a team of Supervisors and licensed 310T Technicians, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring a safe, fully operational transit fleet in a high-demand environment.
While this role is primarily based in Concord, travel between three additional North Toronto locations will be required as needed to support operations.
This position is ideal for a mechanically strong leader who enjoys managing teams, driving performance, and working closely with internal stakeholders and clients to maintain high service standards.

What will your day look like?
Oversee all fleet maintenance operations, ensuring buses are safe, compliant, clean, and road-ready
Drive maximum fleet uptime and availability to support service delivery
Ensure all maintenance activities align with company policies, procedures, and contractual obligations
Identify process gaps and implement improvements to enhance efficiency and performance
Collaborate with the leadership team to address operational challenges and improve service levels
Lead hiring, scheduling, performance management, and retention of mechanics and apprentices
Ensure all technicians maintain required licensing and complete ongoing training
Oversee preventative maintenance programs, inspections, and major repair initiatives (HVAC, engine work, etc.)
Manage and track maintenance claims and related documentation
Oversee multiple functions including Maintenance, Body Shop, Parts, Facilities, and Cleaning teams
Ensure administrative tasks are completed accurately (invoicing, reporting, KPI tracking, personnel updates)
Drive productivity, accountability, and continuous improvement across the operation
Support additional operational requirements as needed
What do you need to qualify for this opportunity?
Licensed 310T Truck & Coach Technician (required) with strong hands-on mechanical background
7+ years of experience within a commercial fleet maintenance environment (truck, bus or coach), with 3+ years of experience managing a fleet or maintenance operation
Strong leadership, organizational, and team management skills
Calm, solutions-focused mindset with strong communication abilities
Comfortable using maintenance management systems and general computer applications
Proficient computer skills (MS Office programs and preventative maintenance software).
Willing and able to support hands-on work when required

What can we offer you?
An annual salary of $100,000 - 130,000 plus discretionary bonus
Comprehensive benefits & RRSP matching
Standard 40-hour work week (day shift), with flexibility required based on operational needs
An opportunity to play a critical leadership role in shaping fleet performance, reliability, and service delivery. 

Please note: This is a fleet/mechanical maintenance leadership role. Candidates must have direct experience managing a commercial fleet maintenance operation. Applicants from building or warehouse maintenance backgrounds will not be considered.

Interested in learning more?
For consideration, please submit your resume ASAP. Although we would like to thank all that apply, only qualified applicants will be contacted.

title: 310T Technician – Afternoon Shift (UNIONIZED)

description:
Automotive, Truck or Heavy Equipment Mechanics - Tired of being stuck in the same shop every day?
 
This Road Service Technician role offers the opportunity to get out on the road, work independently, and take ownership of your territory while servicing a wide range of light and heavy-duty equipment in real-time customer environments.  This position is with a national leader in industrial equipment sales and service, supporting customers across a high-demand service territory, Saskatoon, SK.
 
What will your day look like?
Travel to customer sites to diagnose, repair, and maintain their fleet of industrial equipment
Perform preventative maintenance and safety inspections
Troubleshoot mechanical and electrical issues on battery and engine-powered equipment
Identify required parts and complete repairs efficiently
Document work performed, including diagnostics, repairs, and follow-up actions
Communicate clearly with customers on issues, timelines, and recommendations
Ensure all work is completed in line with safety standards and service expectations
Build strong relationships with customers through professional, reliable service
 
What do you need to qualify for this opportunity?
2+ years of experience as a licensed Automotive, Truck or Heavy-Duty Technician
Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting skills
Comfortable using diagnostic tools, hand tools, and measuring devices
Ability to read and interpret technical manuals and procedures
Strong communication and customer service skills
Valid driver’s license and ability to travel to client sites
 
What can we offer you?
A salary in the range of $70,000 – 80,000 plus O/T available
Comprehensive benefits package
Company pension matching
Company vehicle, gas card, tools and boot allowance
Ongoing training and development
Long-term career growth with a stable, well-established organization
A supportive environment where you’re trusted to manage your work
If you’re a licensed technician looking for more independence, strong earning potential, and the opportunity to work on a variety of industrial equipment, submit your resume ASAP.
 
Although we’d like to thank all that apply confidentially or reach out to learn more.

title: Heavy Equipment Technician
description:
Certus Technical Search is working with a long-standing Canadian equipment provider with 45+ years in business to hire a licensed Heavy Equipment Technician for their Oakville location.
This is a very stable opportunity with a company that invests in its people, offers strong compensation, and provides long-term career growth, not a revolving-door environment.
You’ll be working on a mix of heavy equipment, including aerial equipment and cranes. No prior aerial or crane experience is required. If you’re proficient working on heavy equipment, they will train you.

What can we offer you?
$45.00 - $50.00/hr
Strong benefits and a company pension
$1,000 tool allowance + $250 boot allowance
Casual, respectful shop, no micromanaging
Long-term stability with a company that retains their staff
What will your day-to-day look like?
Shop and field-based diagnostics, repairs, and maintenance
Troubleshooting hydraulic, electrical, mechanical, and powertrain systems
Preventative maintenance, inspections, and repairs done right
Clean, organized shop with proper tooling
What They’re Looking For:
Valid 421A or 310T license
Clean driver's abstract
Strong hands-on experience with heavy equipment
Open to learning new equipment (training provided)
Willing to support occasional field service requirements
Reliable, detail-oriented, and takes pride in producing quality work
If you’re a 421A mechanic looking for steady hours, good pay, and a shop that’s run properly, this one’s worth a look. Submit your resume ASAP to learn more.
Although we would like to thank all applicants, only qualified candidates will be contacted.

Comfortable working both independently and within a team

AZ or DZ license is an asset (not required)

Comprehensive benefits (medical, dental, vision)

$1,200 annual tool allowance + boot allowance

title: 310T Technician – Early Day Shift (UNIONIZED)

description:
Certus Technical Search is working with a major transit operator to hire a Fleet Maintenance Manager for their Concord location.

This is a key leadership role where you will directly impact fleet reliability, service delivery, and overall operational performance. Leading a team of Supervisors and licensed 310T Technicians, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring a safe, fully operational transit fleet in a high-demand environment.

While this role is primarily based in Concord, travel between three additional North Toronto locations will be required as needed to support operations.
This position is ideal for a mechanically strong leader who enjoys managing teams, driving performance, and working closely with internal stakeholders and clients to maintain high service standards.

What will your day look like?
Oversee all fleet maintenance operations, ensuring buses are safe, compliant, clean, and road-ready
Drive maximum fleet uptime and availability to support service delivery
Ensure all maintenance activities align with company policies, procedures, and contractual obligations
Identify process gaps and implement improvements to enhance efficiency and performance
Collaborate with the leadership team to address operational challenges and improve service levels
Lead hiring, scheduling, performance management, and retention of mechanics and apprentices
Ensure all technicians maintain required licensing and complete ongoing training
Oversee preventative maintenance programs, inspections, and major repair initiatives (HVAC, engine work, etc.)
Manage and track maintenance claims and related documentation
Oversee multiple functions including Maintenance, Body Shop, Parts, Facilities, and Cleaning teams
Ensure administrative tasks are completed accurately (invoicing, reporting, KPI tracking, personnel updates)
Drive productivity, accountability, and continuous improvement across the operation
Support additional operational requirements as needed
What do you need to qualify for this opportunity?
Licensed 310T Truck & Coach Technician (required) with strong hands-on mechanical background
7+ years of experience within a commercial fleet maintenance environment (truck, bus or coach), with 3+ years of experience managing a fleet or maintenance operation
Strong leadership, organizational, and team management skills
Calm, solutions-focused mindset with strong communication abilities
Comfortable using maintenance management systems and general computer applications
Proficient computer skills (MS Office programs and preventative maintenance software).
Willing and able to support hands-on work when required

What can we offer you?
An annual salary of $100,000 - 130,000 plus discretionary bonus
Comprehensive benefits & RRSP matching
Standard 40-hour work week (day shift), with flexibility required based on operational needs
An opportunity to play a critical leadership role in shaping fleet performance, reliability, and service delivery. 

Please note: This is a fleet/mechanical maintenance leadership role. Candidates must have direct experience managing a commercial fleet maintenance operation. Applicants from building or warehouse maintenance backgrounds will not be considered.
Interested in learning more?
For consideration, please submit your resume ASAP. Although we would like to thank all that apply, only qualified applicants will be contacted.

title: 310T Technician – Afternoon Shift (UNIONIZED)

description:
If you’re a Red Seal or Journeyperson Technician looking for steady, long-term work in a well-run shop, then this is worth a look.
Certus Technical Search has partnered with a large, established transportation fleet in Winnipeg that is growing their maintenance team. With a fleet of thousands of tractors and trailers in operation, this is a busy, stable environment with consistent year-round work.
The shop is clean, organized, and fully supported by wash bay and maintenance staff, allowing technicians to focus on what they do best.
What’s in it for you:
$33.00 - $45.00/hr. (further flexible for strong technicians)
4-day work week - 10 hour shifts (days or afternoon shifts available)
RRSP matching
Annual tool allowance and boot allowance
Comprehensive health, dental and vision benefits
Continued education support
 
Shop environment:
24/5 operation with 20+ technicians on site
Strong preventative maintenance focus
Team of Maintenance Assistants handling washing and prep
Collaborative team (must be comfortable mentoring apprentices)
 
What they’re looking for:
Licensed Truck & Transport or Trailer Technician (Journeyperson or Red Seal)
Strong diagnostic and time management skills
Ability to complete safeties
Comfortable entering work orders electronically
Physically capable of typical shop demands
 
PLEASE NOTE: Our client is also open to strong 3rd and 4th year apprentices (with education support/reimbursement for the completion of the apprenticeship program)
If you’re in Winnipeg and even mildly curious, please submit your resume ASAP.
Although we would like to thank all that apply, only qualified applicants will be contacted.
